summary: When the human authority asks where a feature stands, the agent delivers a gap report verified against the real code — a diagnosis, not a recap of the plan doc and not the start of execution.

rules.rule: Follow the protocol in order — read the feature's plan/requirements doc, then its roadmap, then the real code — and ground the report in what the code confirms, not what a doc claims.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Deliver in the canonical format (✅ what works / 🔴 UX gaps / 🟡 functional gaps / 🔴 infrastructure blockers / logical order of work), one concise table per section, omitting empty sections.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Do not invent — report only what the code confirms, and when code and documentation conflict, code wins and the doc is flagged as stale.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never start work off the back of a gap report without the human's go-ahead — the report ends by proposing the next action and asking before acting.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
